content.full_text:
J. ROGERS MYERS
MONTE L WIDDERS
LEIGHTON LEE GIBSON
ROBERT L MARSH
ROBERT L LONG

MYERS, WIDDERS & GIBSON
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
8445 EVERGLADES STREET, SUITE 100
VENTURA, CALIFORNIA 93003
(805) 644-7188
(805) 658-8740

PLEASE REPLY TO
POST OFFICE BOX 7201
VENTURA, CALIFORNIA 93-

TELECOPIER: (805) 644-73

March 12, 1990

I.90.212

Ms. Katherine E. Donovan,
General Counsel
Fair Political Practice
Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for Informal Assistance

Dear Ms. Donovan:

The undersigned represents Staal, Gardner & Dunne, Inc., a California Corporation consisting of geotechnical engineers and geologists ("SG&D"). On behalf of SG&D, I would respectfully request informal assistance with regard to my client's filing obligations under the provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974 (the "Act").

SG&D has, since March 5, 1985, been retained by the City of Simi Valley (the "City") as consulting geotechnical engineers. A copy of SG&D's current agreement with the City is enclosed. In that capacity, SG&D specifically reviewed geotechnical reports prepared by other consultants on behalf of the development permit review process. However, as to provide independent opinions regarding those geotechnical reports, it is making role or authority with respect to the approval or denial of the City proposed project itself. Given without advice, direction or control of the City staff. On the other hand, as indicated here in above, the independent contractual relationship with the City, SG&D is called upon to render its independent and albeit unscheduled, basis.

Currently, the City's Conflict of Interest Code (the "Code") does not include "consultant" as a designation requiring filing and disclosure obligations.

In accordance with the facts presented above, I hereby request, on behalf of SG&D, whether or not it would be considered a "consultant" under the Act in its capacity as contract geotechnical engineers to the City.

Ms. Katherine E. Donovan,
General Counsel                                    -2-                                    March 12, 1990


     In the event that you advise me that, as contract geotechnical
engineers to the City, SGD would be deemed a "consultant" within
the meaning of the Act, please inform me as to SGD's disclosure
and filing obligations in view of the fact that the Code does not
specify the filing and disclosure obligations of a "consultant".

     If you require additional information in order to render your
assistance, please do not hesitate to contact the undersigned.

                                                Very truly yours,

                                                MYERS, WIDDERS & WATSON

                                                [signature]
                                                Monte L. Widders

MLW:tla
Enclosure

cc:  Mr. Timothy N. Dunne
     Staal, Gardner & Dunne

     Mr. John Torrance, City Attorney
     City of Simi Valley

embedding.qa_text: **MYERS, WIDDERS & GIBSON** ATTORNEYS AT LAW 5425 EVERGLADES STREET, SUITE 100 VENTURA, CALIFORNIA 93003 (805) 644-7188 (805) 656-5740 March 12, 1990 Ms. Katherine E. Donovan, General Counsel Fair Political Practice Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, California 95814 Re: Request for Informal Assistance Dear Ms. Donovan: The undersigned represents Staal, Gardner & Dunne, Inc., a California corporation consisting of geotechnical engineers and geologists ("SG&D"). On behalf of SG&D, I would respectfully request informal assistance pursuant to regulation section 18329(c) with regard to my client's filing obligations under the provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974 (the "Act"). SG&D has, since March 5, 1985, been retained by the City of Simi Valley (the "City") as consulting geotechnical engineers. A copy of SG&D's current agreement with the City is enclosed for your convenience. In that capacity, SG&D specifically reviews geotechnical reports prepared by other consultants on private development projects as part of the development permit review process. It is asked to provide independent opinions regarding those geotechnical reports. However, SG&D has no specific discretion or decision-making role or authority with respect to the approval or denial of the proposed project itself. The opinions rendered by SG&D to the City are given without advice, direction or control of the City staff. On the other hand, as indicated hereinabove, the independent contractual relationship with the City is long-standing and SG&D is called upon to render its independent advice on a regular, albeit unscheduled, basis. Currently, the City's Conflict of Interest Code (the "Code") does not include "consultant" as a designated employee classification requiring filing and disclosure obligations. In accordance with the facts presented above, I hereby request, on behalf of SG&D, whether or not it would be considered a "consultant" under the Act in its capacity as contract geotechnical engineers to the City. Ms. Katherine E. Donovan, General Counsel March 12, 1990 In the event that you advise me that, as contract geotechnical engineers to the City, SG&D would be deemed a "consultant" within the meaning of the Act, please inform me as to SG&D's disclosure and filing obligations in view of the fact that the Code does not specify the filing and disclosure obligations of a "consultant". If you require additional information in order to render your assistance, please do not hesitate to contact the undersigned. Very truly yours, MYERS, WIDDERS & GIBSON [signature] Monte L. Widders MLW:tla Enclosure cc: Mr. Timothy N. Dunne Staal, Gardner & Dunne Mr. John Torrance, City Attorney City of Simi Valley
